rulings.Case Title: ABRAHAM CASTILLO ET AL VS. CLEAVER-BROOKS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY JUDGMENT / Notice Of Motion For Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for August 24, 2016, Wednesday, in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Cleaver-Brooks, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of supporting the premise for its motion: that "the sole boiler at the sole site at issue did not incorporate any asbestos-containing parts." Plaintiffs' objections to defendant's responses to requests for production (Exhibit G) and to the declaration of John Tornetta (Exhibit N) are sustained. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: ABRAHAM CASTILLO ET AL VS. CLEAVER-BROOKS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for August 24, 2016, Wednesday, in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant Fraser's Boiler Service, Inc.'s motion for summary judgment is granted. Defendant sustained its initial burden. Plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether Mr. Castillo was exposed to asbestos-containing products or materials attributable to defendant. Specifically, plaintiffs failed to present admissible evidence to create a triable issue whether the materials to which defendant's employees exposed Mr. Castillo aboard the USS Sperry contained asbestos. Based on this conclusion, the Court did not reach defendant's argument regarding substantial factor causation. Plaintiffs' objections to Bruce Fraser's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to Mr. Castillo's declaration are overruled. Defendant's objections to Charles Ay's declaration are sustained as to paragraphs 49, 50 and 51 and overruled as to all other objections. The Court did not consider the rough draft transcript of Mr. Ay's deposition submitted with defendant's reply papers. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)

rulings.Case Title: ABRAHAM CASTILLO ET AL VS. CLEAVER-BROOKS, INC. ET AL
rulings.Rulings: On Asbestos Law and Motion Calendar for August 24, 2016, Wednesday, in Department 503, Line 1. Defendant R.F. MacDonald's motion for summary judgment is denied. Defendant failed to sustain its initial burden. Defendant failed to sustain its burden of supporting the premise for its motion as stated by defendant: "R.F. MacDonald did not perform any work at Mr. Castillo's workplace until 2002. R.F. MacDonald serviced the Cleaver-Brooks boiler at Mr. Castillo's workplace 2002-2013 and did not service a Dixon boiler. R.F. MacDonald used the materials specified by Cleaver-Brooks; the companies that manufactured parest (sic) appropriate for use on Cleaver-Brooks boilers discontinued the use of asbestos in their products prior to 1990. R.F. MacDonald did not supply asbestos-containing parts the (sic) Mr. Castillo's workplace." (Reply, 2:25-3:3.) Plaintiffs' objections to John Tornetta's declaration (Exhibit N) and to James MacDonald's declaration (Exhibit O) are sustained. Plaintiffs' opposition is timely pursuant to the August 9, 2016 stipulation and order dictating notice and briefing schedule for defendant's motion. If a hearing is requested, it will be at 9:30a.m. A court reporter will not be provided by the court. If any party wishes to have the matter reported, the parties must meet and confer to agree on only one court reporter for any reported matter in this case. There will be only one official record in a case. The designated reporter must be licensed pursuant to Bus. & Prof. Code, Sec. 8016. If the parties are unable to agree on one reporter, the court will designate a qualified court reporter that will provide the official transcript for the matter. The party or parties shall pay for the cost of reporter. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestasbestostr@sftc.org by 4:30pm stating their intention to contest. If a tentative ruling is contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing. If a tentative ruling is not contested, the prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must either bring the proposed order to the hearing or submit it after the hearing in compliance with CRC 3.1312. = (503/GLW)
